---
title: "Pros and Cons of Use vs. Recovery"
format: html
---
# Pros and Cons of Use vs. Recovery

Ambivalence is normal in behavior change. You may genuinely want recovery and still feel pulled toward alcohol in difficult moments. A structured pros-and-cons exercise helps you examine both sides honestly so choices are based on full information, not just immediate urge.

This tool is most effective when you compare **short-term and long-term** consequences.

Decisional balance is especially useful during mixed feelings because it externalizes competing motives. Seeing short-term and long-term consequences side by side improves clarity during craving episodes.

## Decisional Balance Worksheet
| | Using alcohol | Staying in recovery |
|---|---|---|
| Short-term benefits |  |  |
| Short-term costs |  |  |
| Long-term benefits |  |  |
| Long-term costs |  |  |

::: {.callout-important}
### Practice plan

Work through this in real-life situations rather than only reading it.

Review your worksheet when bargaining thoughts appear.

If something feels hard, simplify the step and repeat it consistently.
:::
